summary: Stancil argues that Silicon Valley used to run on familiar boom-bust cycles, but the AI era is breaking the old “physics,” replacing predictable rhythms with frantic rebranding, uncertain value, and career anxiety. The result is a tech industry that outwardly projects confidence yet privately feels unmoored, unsure whether this is another wave or a structural shift.
money_quotes: In Silicon Valley, there are two sets of physics.
But in private, the vibe is different.
There are no reliable physics anymore.
key_insight: AI has turned Silicon Valley’s once-cyclical, legible boom-bust dynamics into a confusing, destabilizing moment where even veterans doubt the rules of the game.
